title: Dress-Up Closet from Stair Spindles
summary: A miniature open closet built around two turned stair spindles instead of plain lumber posts — jigsaw-cut corbels and curtain rod finials dress it up further.
description:
The whole personality of this build comes from repurposing stock stair spindles as the vertical posts instead of milling or turning your own — an easy way to get a decorative turned profile without a lathe. Curtain rod finials cap the tops for the same reason: real turned hardware, bought rather than made, standing in for a much harder woodworking operation.

The corbels are the one part that does need cutting from scratch, with a jigsaw, and they serve double duty — visually finishing the transition from post to shelf, and physically supporting the shelf boards along with the 2x4 base. Get the corbel shape roughed out and test-fit against the spindle before committing to the final cut; a corbel that doesn't sit flush against the post's turned profile is the one place a small gap will be obvious.

tools.note: Cuts the corbel shapes and drills out the finial holes.

materials.name: Pine, 1x2 or closet rod pole, 3 ft

cutList.note: Both ends cut at 30°, ends not parallel — long point to long point.

steps.title: Build the base
steps.body: Attach the 2x4 base pieces together using a fastener between 2-1/2" and 2-3/4" long. If nailing, use glue as well.

steps.title: Attach the stair spindles
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes to attach each stair spindle to the base, one on each side. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Cut and attach the corbels
steps.body:
Cut the corbel shapes from the 1x6 with a jigsaw, test-fitting against the spindle's turned profile before finalizing the cut — a corbel that doesn't sit flush leaves a visible gap. Attach with 3/4" pocket holes to both the spindle and the 2x4 base.

Drill out the top of each spindle for the curtain rod finials and attach.

steps.title: Add the shelf and closet rod
steps.body: Attach the shelf boards between the corbels with screws into the 2x4 base and corbels. Measure the distance between the two spindles at the top and cut the closet rod (or 1x2) to fit. Attach with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue.
materials: Pine, 1x2 or closet rod pole, 3 ft

images.alt: Dress-up closet built from turned stair spindles
